basics.name: Marcus Chen
basics.label: Site Reliability Engineer
basics.summary: SRE with a passion for observability, incident response, and building platforms that stay up when everything else is on fire. Former backend engineer turned reliability advocate.

work.name: StreamVault
work.description: Video infrastructure SaaS serving 2B+ monthly API requests
work.summary: Own reliability for core ingestion pipeline and global CDN edge configuration.
highlights: Cut P99 latency 38% by redesigning autoscaling policies on Kubernetes.
Introduced SLO/error-budget culture; reduced customer-facing incidents by 52% YoY.
Led postmortem program adopted company-wide with blameless review templates.

work.name: DataPipe Inc.
work.description: Enterprise ETL and data pipeline platform
work.summary: Built internal developer platform on AWS EKS with Terraform and Helm.
highlights: Migrated 40+ microservices from EC2 to EKS with zero planned downtime windows.
Implemented OpenTelemetry tracing across 100% of production services.

volunteer.summary: Reviewed talk proposals and curated reliability track sessions.
highlights: Moderated opening keynote panel on AI-assisted incident response.

awards.title: Golden SLO Award
awards.summary: Awarded for achieving 99.99% availability across ingestion tier for four consecutive quarters.

publications.name: Error Budgets That Engineers Actually Use
publications.summary: Practical patterns for tying product priorities to reliability investment without dashboard fatigue.

projects.name: OnCall Roulette
projects.description: Fair rotation scheduler with timezone-aware handoffs and PTO blackout support.
highlights: Exports iCal feeds and Slack reminders.

projects.name: SLO Burn Talk
projects.description: Conference talk on multi-window burn-rate alerting for tier-1 services.

references.name: Lisa Park
references.reference: Marcus turned our reliability culture from reactive firefighting into measurable engineering discipline. He is exceptional at teaching SLO thinking to product teams.
